general_info.definition: Na-functionalized carbon quantum dots used as additives in thin film nanocomposite (TFN) membranes

method.conditions: 180°C for 3 hours in a glass container with loose cover

post_treatment: Dialysis using Slide-A-Lyzer G2 Dialysis Cassettes (2K MWCO)
For Na-CQDs: neutralization with 5.0 M NaOH to pH 8 before freeze-drying

structural.morphology: well dispersed without agglomeration and have a nearly spherical shape

applications.domain: Membrane technology for brackish water desalination
applications.performance: TFN membrane with 1 wt% Na-CQDs: Pure water permeability = 2.56 LMH/bar (+47.1% vs TFC), NaCl rejection = 97.7%. After 23 bar stabilization: Pure water permeability = 4.27 LMH/bar, NaCl rejection = 98.6%.
applications.comparative_advantage: Na-CQDs increase membrane roughness (Rq: 96.8 nm vs 60.2 nm for TFC), effective surface area (46.1 μm² vs 38.1 μm²), and hydrophilicity. They create interstitial spaces in polyamide chains.
